texto: There is no formula to relationships. They have to be negotiated in loving ways, with room for both parties, what they want and what they need, what they can do and what their life is like. In business, people negotiate to win. They negotiate to get what they want. Maybe you’re too used to that. Love is different. Love is when you are as concerned about someone else’s situation as you are about your own.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Morrie Schwartz: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “There is no formula to relationships. They have to be negotiated in loving ways, with room for bo…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que negocios, concern, formula pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de formula e love.

texto: Death ends a life, not a relationship. All the love you created is still there. All the memories are still there. You live on- in the hearts of everyone you have touched and nurtured while you were here.
